testcase.name: RateLimit decorator > attaches route metadata
InMemoryRateLimitMetrics > tracks blocked counters, latency percentiles, and histogram buckets
PgRateLimitStore > validates configured SQL identifiers
PgRateLimitStore > returns an allowed local decision without a tenant
PgRateLimitStore > upserts distributed windows and maps allowed and blocked decisions
DatabaseRateLimitPolicyResolver > uses explicit metadata.limit and metadata.windowSeconds without touching the DB
DatabaseRateLimitPolicyResolver > falls back to per-scope defaults from options when DB returns no rows
DatabaseRateLimitPolicyResolver > falls back to defaultLimit / defaultWindowSeconds when no per-scope default
DatabaseRateLimitPolicyResolver > falls back to the hardcoded 120 / 60 when no defaults are configured
DatabaseRateLimitPolicyResolver > uses tenant override when present
DatabaseRateLimitPolicyResolver > falls back to platform config (core.config) when no override
DatabaseRateLimitPolicyResolver > tries the colon platform config key when the dot key is not present
DatabaseRateLimitPolicyResolver > skips override lookup when tenantId is undefined
DatabaseRateLimitPolicyResolver > returns nulls (then falls back) when no Database is wired
DatabaseRateLimitPolicyResolver > defaults cost to 1 when metadata.cost is undefined
RateLimitGuard > bypasses health-check routes without touching the store
RateLimitGuard > bypasses routes marked as @System()
RateLimitGuard > applies headers and allows when the store accepts the request
RateLimitGuard > throws 429, emits Retry-After, and increments metrics on block
RateLimitGuard > allows the request when no rate-limit metadata is set on the handler
RateLimitGuard > uses default options when only the reflector is provided
RateLimitGuard > allows the request when no policyResolver is wired even if metadata is set
RateLimitGuard > open-circuits to allow when the store is missing
RateLimitGuard > open-circuits to allow when the store throws a non-Http error
RateLimitGuard > rethrows when the store throws ServiceUnavailableException
RateLimitGuard > throws 503 under distributedStrict when the store returns null (resolver missing)
RateLimitGuard > open-circuits on backend errors when distributedStrict is explicitly false
RateLimitGuard > builds bucket keys for ip / user / route buckets
RateLimitGuard > builds fallback bucket keys from originalUrl, url, defaults, and alternate principals
RateLimitGuard > allows requests without a response object while still consuming the store
RateLimitGuard > extracts tenantId + userId from a Bearer JWT payload as fallback
RateLimitGuard > ignores malformed Bearer tokens silently
RateLimitGuard > ignores Bearer tokens whose payload is not valid JSON
RateLimitGuard > ignores non-string JWT subject and tenant claims
RateLimitGuard > ignores non-Bearer and non-string authorization headers
RateLimitGuard > accepts authorization header passed as an array (takes first element)
RedisSlidingWindowRateLimitStore > does not initialize a Redis client without redis options
RedisSlidingWindowRateLimitStore > loads the sliding-window script, maps Redis numeric responses, and quits open clients
RedisSlidingWindowRateLimitStore > skips quit for closed clients
